questions.q: A crystal of KCl containing a small amount of $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ will have
options: vacant $\mathrm{K}^{+}$sites and a higher density as compared to pure KCl
vacant $\mathrm{K}^{+}$sites and a lower density as compared to pure KCl

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A scientist attempts to replace a few carbon atoms in 1.0 g of diamond with boron atoms or nitrogen atoms in separate experiments. Which of the following is correct?
options: The resulting material with B doping will be an n -type semiconductor
The resulting material with $B$ doping will be a p-type semiconductor
B doping is NOT possible as B cannot form multiple bonds
The resulting material with N doping will be a p-type semiconductor

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Read carefully all the three statements on defects in solids:
(i) In Frenkel defect, interstitial $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$site is surrounded tetrahedrally by four $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ions and four $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$ions, where interstitial $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$interaction is covalent
(ii) Addition of small amount of $\mathrm{SrCl}_2$ in NaCl yields solid solution with a formula of $\mathrm{Na}_{1-2 \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{Sr}_{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{V}_{\text {Nax }} \mathrm{Cl}$, where $\mathrm{V}=$ valency
(iii) In general, Schottky defect increases the density of the substance
Choose the correct alternative(s).
